novel_features.name: Site Snapshot & Diff
novel_features.description: Track your Framer project's evolution over time with full structural snapshots and visual diffs
novel_features.rationale: Requires local SQLite persistence across time — MCP tools are stateless and forget everything after each call
novel_features.example: framer-pp-cli snapshot --label 'before-redesign' && framer-pp-cli diff latest~1 latest --json
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to understand what changed in a project between two points in time, this is the only way

novel_features.name: Multi-Project Dashboard
novel_features.description: Query across all registered Framer projects at once — stale CMS, unpublished changes, collection health
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local registry of N project configs — all existing tools bind to a single project
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent manages multiple client sites and needs a single command to find which projects need attention

novel_features.name: CMS Sync with Dry-Run
novel_features.description: Import CMS content from CSV/JSON/Sheets with a preview diff before committing changes
novel_features.rationale: Computes a local diff between source data and remote CMS state — MCP tools are fire-and-forget with no preview
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to bulk-update CMS content safely without risking accidental overwrites or deletions

novel_features.name: Migration Scaffold
novel_features.description: Scrape an existing website and generate a complete Framer migration plan with pages, content, and assets
novel_features.rationale: No existing tool bridges arbitrary websites to Framer projects — combines web scraping with Framer API operations
novel_features.example: framer-pp-cli migrate-scrape https://old-site.com --depth 3 && framer-pp-cli migrate-apply manifest.json --dry-run
novel_features.why_it_matters: When porting an existing site to Framer, this automates the most tedious part of the migration

novel_features.name: Silent Failure Guard
novel_features.description: Read-back verification on every mutation catches Framer's silent attribute rejections
novel_features.rationale: Requires a stateful client that caches intended state and compares after write — MCP tools fire and forget
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent sets node attributes and needs to know whether they actually took effect

novel_features.name: CMS Schema Diff
novel_features.description: Declare CMS schema in YAML and diff it against the live project — infrastructure-as-code for Framer CMS
novel_features.rationale: Treats CMS schema as diffable infrastructure — no existing tool supports schema-as-code for Framer
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to verify CMS schema matches the expected structure before syncing content

novel_features.name: Bulk Asset Pipeline
novel_features.description: Upload a directory of images and auto-bind them to CMS items by filename-to-slug matching
novel_features.rationale: Batch upload with CMS field mapping in one command — existing tools handle single assets only
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to upload dozens of images and link them to the right CMS records in one operation

novel_features.name: Publish Guardrails
novel_features.description: Pre-publish linting catches broken links, missing CMS references, orphan pages, and empty text nodes
novel_features.rationale: Full project graph traversal against a local snapshot — no existing tool does site-wide validation before publish
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent is about to deploy and needs confidence that the site won't ship with broken references

novel_features.name: Localization Sync
novel_features.description: Push and pull translations between standard i18n formats (CSV, PO, XLIFF) and Framer's localization system
novel_features.rationale: Bridges standard translation toolchains to Framer's proprietary localization model
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent manages multi-language sites and needs to sync translations from external translation tools

novel_features.name: Redirect Map Generator
novel_features.description: Auto-generate redirect map by crawling old site's sitemap and fuzzy-matching to Framer page slugs
novel_features.rationale: Combines external sitemap crawl with Framer page introspection and fuzzy URL matching
novel_features.why_it_matters: Every site migration needs redirects — this automates what is otherwise a fully manual spreadsheet task

novel_features.name: CMS Relationship Validator
novel_features.description: Find broken collection references, orphan items, and circular refs across your CMS
novel_features.rationale: Graph traversal across collections using local snapshot — no tool validates Framer CMS referential integrity
novel_features.why_it_matters: When CMS grows past 3-4 collections, broken references become invisible — this surfaces them

novel_features.name: Design Token Import
novel_features.description: Import CSS variables or Tailwind config as Framer color and text styles — no manual recreation
novel_features.rationale: Parses CSS/Tailwind locally and creates Framer styles via API — no tool bridges design token formats to Framer
novel_features.why_it_matters: When porting a site with an existing design system, this eliminates hours of manual style recreation

novel_features.name: Code File Sync
novel_features.description: Edit TSX components locally in your editor, then push to Framer — eliminates copy-paste workflow
novel_features.rationale: Two-way file sync between local filesystem and Framer code files — no tool offers local editing with push/pull
novel_features.example: framer-pp-cli code-pull HeroSection -o hero.tsx && vim hero.tsx && framer-pp-cli code-push hero.tsx --name HeroSection
novel_features.why_it_matters: When a developer needs to edit Framer components in their preferred editor without the copy-paste dance

narrative.headline: Every Framer operation from your terminal — CMS sync, bulk upload, site migration, and a local database no other Framer tool has
narrative.value_prop: Framer's Server API is powerful but locked behind a JavaScript SDK with no CLI. framer-pp-cli wraps every API method with offline search, dry-run previews, multi-project management, and migration automation that turns site porting from days into hours.
narrative.auth_narrative: Framer uses per-project API keys generated in Site Settings. The CLI stores project profiles locally so you can switch between projects with --project aliases. Requires Node.js 18+ and the framer-api npm package as a runtime bridge.

quickstart.comment: Preview a CMS import before committing

troubleshoots.fix: Verify FRAMER_API_KEY is valid and Node.js 18+ is installed: framer-pp-cli doctor
Use --dry-run to preview the request before sending: framer-pp-cli nodes set NODE_ID --dry-run
Install the npm package globally: npm install -g framer-api
Ensure your CSV has a unique 'slug' column matching existing item slugs for upsert behavior

narrative.when_to_use: Use framer-pp-cli when you need to automate Framer operations from scripts, CI/CD pipelines, or AI agents. Especially valuable for site migrations (importing content, uploading assets, generating redirects), multi-project management, and CMS content pipelines. Not for visual design work — use the Framer editor for that.

recipes.title: Bulk import blog posts from CSV
recipes.command: framer-pp-cli cms-sync ./posts.csv --collection Blog --dry-run && framer-pp-cli cms-sync ./posts.csv --collection Blog
recipes.explanation: Preview the import diff first, then commit the changes

recipes.title: Port design tokens from Tailwind
recipes.explanation: Import your entire Tailwind color palette as Framer styles

recipes.title: Edit a component locally
recipes.explanation: Pull a Framer code file to edit locally, then push back with code-push

recipes.title: Generate migration redirects
recipes.explanation: Preview redirect map generation from old site sitemap

recipes.title: Pre-publish health check
recipes.explanation: Create a preview deployment and get the shareable URL
